message:
feat: vision side-channel — off-loop image description via purpose-driven API call

When the agent reads an image file, a separate API call with no tools
asks the model to describe what it sees. The calling LLM provides
context via a new `purpose` parameter on read_file ("What do you hope
to learn by looking at this image?"), and the side-channel uses that
purpose as its prompt. The description is injected back into the
conversation as a steering message.

This solves the fundamental vision problem: GPT-5.4 in tool-calling
mode never describes images — it immediately writes PIL/template-matching
code. The side-channel forces native vision (no tools available), and
the LLM-driven purpose ensures task-relevant descriptions.

Results: chess-best-move went from 0/3 baseline → 1/3 with prompt/code
fixes → 6/6 correct with the vision side-channel (two runs of 3 reps).

Also includes:
- `purpose` field on read_file tool definition
- `Purpose` field on ImageResult and ToolExecResult
- Explorer agents skip the side-channel (just inventorying files)
- detail:"original" for GPT-5.4+ in the side-channel call

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
